assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 25-29 show a JQL search returning TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'. Lines 33-44 present a concurrent triage warning identifying quinn-proto as the shared upstream component. The heading is 'Step 7 -- Concurrent Triage Detection'.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 36-39 contain the warning table with columns 'CVE Issue | Status | Assignee' showing 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Both the issue key and assignee are explicitly included in the warning.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 48-67 present exactly three options: (1) Wait -- Pause until TC-8019's triage completes, (2) Skip -- Skip remediation task creation entirely with a Jira comment, (3) Proceed -- Create remediation tasks with the 'concurrent-triage-overlap' label. All three match the assertion requirements.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 48 states 'Three options are offered before proceeding to Case A/B/C branching in Step 8'. Lines 85-87 confirm 'The engineer must select one of the three options before triage can continue to Step 8 (Remediation).' triage-outcome.md line 93 confirms 'The triage is paused at Step 7 pending the engineer's choice' and lines 96-107 list Step 7 as completed while remediation tasks (Case A/B) remain pending.
